native_style_cli_aliases: On Windows, argv tokens starting with `/` after the subcommand are rewritten to GNU-style flags before clap parses (e.g. `verify /pa /q file.exe`, `verify /vr`, `verify /p7s path.p7s`, `verify /testroot`, `verify /sl`, `verify /ph` pairs with `/v` like native, `sign /fd SHA256 /tseal URL file`, `sign /sa OID value`, `sign /seal`, `sign /c TemplateName`, `timestamp /force`, `remove /s file.exe`). Additionally, signtool-windows accepts `--` long aliases mirroring native names (sign `--fd`, `--tr`, `--tseal`, `--c`, `--sa`, `--fdchw`, `--tdchw`, `--rmc`, `--seal`, `--itos`, `--force`, `--nosealwarn`, `--noenclavewarn`; verify `--tw`, `--kp`, `--vr`, `--sha1`, `--p7s`, `--testroot`, `--sl`, `--bp`, `--enclave`; timestamp `--tr`/`--td`/`--tp`, `--force`, `--nosealwarn`; catdb `--d`/`--g`/`--r`/`--u`; remove `--s`/`--c`/`--u`).

invocation.notes: UTF-8 (optional BOM) or UTF-16 LE/BE with BOM; invalid UTF-8 falls back to UTF-16 LE without BOM; one argument per line; double-quoted lines with "" escapes; blank line separates commands when @file is the only tail arg; inline @path splices one block; `@@` prefix strips one `@` for a literal leading at-sign; native signtool may mis-parse @ when the signtool.exe path contains spaces—parity script uses a TEMP copy

sign.notes: DER/serialized cert file merged into signing store
Sign-time sealing removal; explicit not-implemented (timestamp also has --force)
Returns explicit error; use native signtool for split digest
Explicit error; PKCS#7 product signing differs from PE SIP
Sign-time sealed RFC3161 URL; mutually exclusive with /tr and /t; same SignerSignEx3 RFC3161 path as /tr here
Post-sign PE Authenticode digest vs PKCS#7 indirect digest after `SignerSignEx3`; `SIGNTOOL_RS_RUST_SIP=pe`; `/rust-sip pe` native argv; not production SIP replacement
`pe` / `script` / `msi` / `esd` / `msix` / `cab` / `catalog` enable digest gates; `off` disables env; `--rust-sip off` overrides
Post-sign script digest vs PKCS#7: PowerShell UTF-16 strip (pwrshsip); WSH strip + offset dword (wshext); COM text decode may differ
Post-sign MSI OLE fingerprint vs PKCS#7 indirect digest after `SignerSignEx3` (Signify-compatible `sip_rust::msi_digest`); `/rust-sip msi` argv; only when target resolves to Windows Installer format
Post-sign WIM/ESD prefix digest vs PKCS#7 (`sip_rust::esd_digest`, `EsdSip.dll` semantics); `/rust-sip esd` argv; `.wim`/`.esd` only
Cleartext MSIX/APPX/bundles only; post-sign ZIP-rehash digest vs PKCS#7 (`sip_rust::msix_digest`); `/rust-sip msix`; encrypted `.eappx`/`.emsix` rejected
Post-sign CAB Authenticode digest vs PKCS#7 (`sip_rust::cab_digest`); `/rust-sip cab`; `.cab` only
Post-sign catalog digest vs PKCS#7 (`sip_rust::catalog_digest`); `/rust-sip catalog`; `.cat` only

verify.notes: Try resolved catalogs then embedded
Subsystem GUID best-effort

Catalog WinTrust only: Rust sets `WTD_USE_DEFAULT_OSVER_CHECK` in `verify_with_catalog`; embedded `/pa` + `/o` is rejected by current signtool unless `/a`/`/c`/… so Rust errors without `--catalog`/`--catalog-search`. Parity: `verify_pa_os_version_check_exit_match`
WTD_REVOCATION_CHECK_CHAIN_EXCLUDE_ROOT when set
Documented no-op; WinVerifyTrust default varies by OS
Same WinTrust file path as PE; PKCS-only semantics limited
SPC_SP_OPUS_INFO from WinVerifyTrust state; requires -v like native

Requires -v like native; WTD_HASH_ONLY_FLAG + OID scan for SPC_PE_IMAGE_PAGE_HASHES; warns exit 2 if absent; portable probe + table parse (no WinTrust): `signtool-portable pe-has-page-hashes` / `pe-page-hash-info` — see `portable_digest_cli`
Repeatable; signer cert SHA1 must match one value
Repeatable; at least one intermediate CA thumbprint must match chain
Verify verb: warn exit 2 if EKU OID missing (distinct from sign --eku-oid)
Content file for detached PKCS#7 verify
Alias p7s for detached PKCS#7 path
Windows argv /testroot supported
Warn exit 2 when PCA 2010 in chain; auto with driver policy or /kp
Suppress PCA 2010 warnings
WinTrust `WSS_VERIFY_SEALING` on embedded verify; not combined with `--catalog` path or detached PKCS#7; parity `verify_pa_sl_exit_match`

CLI + argv; WinTrust Action GUID for biometric policy is not published in `SoftPub.h` / standard SDK headers like `WINTRUST_ACTION_GENERIC_VERIFY_V2` — needs parity against native `signtool.exe` or Microsoft guidance before safe wiring
CLI + argv; same as /bp — dedicated enclave verify Action/policy not exposed in public Authenticode headers used elsewhere in this crate
After embedded WinTrust success on PE/WinMD, recompute Authenticode digest in Rust vs PKCS#7; `/rust-sip-pe-digest-check` argv alias
After WinTrust on signed scripts (PowerShell or WSH), heuristic digest vs PKCS#7; `/rust-sip-script-digest-check` argv alias
After WinTrust on signed `.msi`, Rust MSI SIP fingerprint vs PKCS#7 + optional `MsiDigitalSignatureEx` pre-hash; `/rust-sip-msi-digest-check` argv alias
After WinTrust on signed `.wim`/`.esd`, Rust ESD SIP prefix digest vs PKCS#7; `/rust-sip-esd-digest-check` argv alias
Cleartext packages only; after WinTrust success, Rust MSIX ZIP digest vs PKCS#7; `/rust-sip-msix-digest-check`; encrypted extensions explicit error
After WinTrust on signed `.cab`, Rust CAB digest vs PKCS#7; `/rust-sip-cab-digest-check`
After WinTrust on `.cat`, Rust catalog digest vs PKCS#7; `/rust-sip-catalog-digest-check`
Enables every `--rust-sip-*-digest-check` flag for this verify; `/rust-sip-all-digest-checks`; additive to WinVerifyTrust only
Never replaces default WinVerifyTrust; consistency checks only

timestamp.notes: Mutually exclusive with /tr and /t; uses same SignerTimeStampEx3 RFC3161 path as /tr in this build

remove.notes: Global /q (--quiet): no per-file success lines on stdout, matching native; `ImageEnumerateCertificates` uses `CERT_SECTION_TYPE_ANY` (aligned with remove /u path)
CryptMsgControl CMSG_CTRL_DEL_CERT after matching SignerInfo Issuer+Serial to cert bag; PE Image* replace PKCS#7; `/c`+`/u` one CryptMsg session (parity `remove_cu_sha256_match_native`)
CryptMsgControl DEL_SIGNER_UNAUTH_ATTR + PE Image* replace PKCS#7; enumerate uses CERT_SECTION_TYPE_ANY

tier_summary.P0: Common CLI surface aligned with native workflows where feasible
tier_summary.P1: Digest split pipeline and advanced verify metadata — partial by design
tier_summary.P2: Low-volume PKCS7 product modes and rare remove modes

code_sign_file_formats.windows_mechanism: Registered Cryptography SIP for PowerShell / module / manifest files; native `signtool` and `SignerSignEx3` load the OS SIP implementation.
code_sign_file_formats.rust_path: Same Win32 stack (`SignerSignEx3`, `WinVerifyTrust`) — uses the OS SIP DLL; see `src/win/code_sign_format.rs`.
code_sign_file_formats.parity: `scripts/run-parity-diff.ps1`: `.ps1` — `sign_ps1_*`, `verify_ps1_*`, `artifact_verify_ps1_print_description_match` (`/d` `/du` + `verify /v /d` vs `--print-description`; shares `$parityDesc`/`$parityUrl` with PE). `.psm1` / `.psd1` — same plus `artifact_verify_psm1_print_description_match`, `artifact_verify_psd1_print_description_match`. Fixtures under `tests/fixtures/` or env overrides `SIGNTOOL_RS_PS1_UNSIGNED_FIXTURE`, `_PSM1_`, `_PSD1_`. Exact SHA256 when PKCS#7 matches native; else `artifact_semantic_match` if both `/pa` verify succeed.

code_sign_file_formats.windows_mechanism: PE Authenticode (`WIN_CERTIFICATE`); SIP resolves from PE metadata.
code_sign_file_formats.rust_path: Same Win32 SIP delegation as native; optional experimental `--rust-sip pe` post-sign digest gate (`crates/signtool-sip-digest`, re-exported under `sip_rust`).
code_sign_file_formats.parity: `run-parity-diff.ps1` `sign_pe_fixture_sha256_match_native` / `verify_pe_fixture_pa_exit_match` on `SIGNTOOL_RS_UNSIGNED_FIXTURE` (two-copy sign + SHA256 or verify-valid semantic match); plus `artifact_sign_two_pe_exit_parity` and semantic verify scenarios.

code_sign_file_formats.windows_mechanism: Windows metadata files are CLI assemblies in a PE-based container; Authenticode via OS SIP (same `SignerSignEx3` / `WinVerifyTrust` stack as PE signing).
code_sign_file_formats.rust_path: `CodeSignFormat::WindowsMetadata` in `src/win/code_sign_format.rs`; same Win32 delegation as native.
code_sign_file_formats.parity: Optional `scripts/run-parity-diff.ps1` when `SIGNTOOL_RS_WINMD_UNSIGNED_FIXTURE` + `SIGNTOOL_RS_TEST_PFX` set: `sign_winmd_sha256_match_native`, `verify_winmd_pa_exit_match`, `artifact_verify_winmd_print_description_match`; optional `SIGNTOOL_RS_WINMD_TIMESTAMP_URL`. Local smoke: `scripts/sip-format-smoke.ps1`. `remove /s` allowed only for PE-image-backed mapped extensions — `.winmd` is treated as PE-backed (`Image*` APIs) like `.exe`.

code_sign_file_formats.windows_mechanism: AppX/MSIX SIP; tooling often uses decoupled digest `/dlib` + `/dmdf` + `/ph`.
code_sign_file_formats.rust_path: Same Win32 SIP + decoupled digest bridge as native.
code_sign_file_formats.parity: `scripts/msix-parity-sign.ps1` and MSIX blocks in `run-parity-diff.ps1` (`artifact_msix_sign_semantic`, `artifact_msix_decoupled_semantic` when dlib env set). Description parity: `artifact_verify_msix_print_description_match` — sign with `/d` `/du` + RFC3161 (`SIGNTOOL_RS_MSIX_*` env), then `verify /v /d` vs `--print-description` (same strings as PE description test).

code_sign_file_formats.windows_mechanism: Windows Installer SIP (OLE compound storage Authenticode).
code_sign_file_formats.rust_path: `SignerSignEx3` / `WinVerifyTrust` — OS SIP; `code_sign_format::WindowsInstaller`; experimental digest parity in `sip_rust::msi_digest` (`--rust-sip msi`, `verify --rust-sip-msi-digest-check`); `remove` rejects (not PE `Image*`).
code_sign_file_formats.parity: No bundled unsigned `.msi` in-repo (OLE compound); optional parity in `run-parity-diff.ps1` when `SIGNTOOL_RS_MSI_UNSIGNED_FIXTURE` + `SIGNTOOL_RS_TEST_PFX` point at a valid package: `sign_msi_sha256_match_native`, `verify_msi_pa_exit_match`, `artifact_verify_msi_print_description_match`. Optional sign-time RFC3161: `SIGNTOOL_RS_MSI_TIMESTAMP_URL`.

code_sign_file_formats.rust_path: `SignerSignEx3` / `WinVerifyTrust` — OS SIP; `code_sign_format::WimImage`; experimental digest parity in `sip_rust::esd_digest` (`--rust-sip esd`, `verify --rust-sip-esd-digest-check`); `remove` rejects.
code_sign_file_formats.parity: No bundled signed WIM in-repo; optional local sign/verify with native `signtool` then `signtool-windows verify /pa --rust-sip-esd-digest-check` for Rust consistency.

code_sign_file_formats.windows_mechanism: Windows Script Host–related SIP where registered for the extension.
code_sign_file_formats.rust_path: Same Win32 stack as native (`SignerSignEx3`, `WinVerifyTrust`); no in-tree SIP port.
code_sign_file_formats.parity: `run-parity-diff.ps1`: per-extension sign/verify SHA256 + `artifact_verify_{js,vbs,wsf}_print_description_match` (`/d` `/du` then `verify /v /d` vs `--print-description`; same `$parityDesc`/`$parityUrl` as PE). Fixtures `unsigned-sample.{js,vbs,wsf}` or env `SIGNTOOL_RS_JS_UNSIGNED_FIXTURE`, `_VBS_`, `_WSF_`. Requires OS SIP registration per extension.

portable_digest_cli.platforms_note: Builds on Linux/macOS/Windows; no `windows` / WinVerifyTrust dependency.
portable_digest_cli.purpose: Optional digest recomputation vs PKCS#7 indirect data and PE page-hash OID scans — additive to native signtool, not a full replacement.

commands.name: verify-pe
commands.maps_to_native_concept: Consistency check after conceptual WinTrust success — similar to `signtool-windows verify --rust-sip-pe-digest-check`

commands.name: trust-verify-pe
commands.maps_to_native_concept: Explicit-anchor PKCS#7 trust + picky chain + PE digest (not WinTrust); optional `--as-of YYYY-MM-DD` for expired fixtures

commands.name: trust-verify-cab
commands.maps_to_native_concept: `verify-cab` digest consistency then same trust stack as PE

commands.name: trust-verify-catalog
commands.maps_to_native_concept: `verify-catalog` CMS digest consistency then picky trust when Authenticode-wrapped

commands.name: trust-verify-detached
commands.maps_to_native_concept: Detached PKCS#7 vs raw content bytes (digest algo inferred from PKCS#7); blob normalization like Win32 detached helpers

commands.name: pe-has-page-hashes
commands.maps_to_native_concept: Subset of `verify /ph` — CMS signed-attribute probe plus raw OID TLV fallback

commands.name: pe-page-hash-info
commands.maps_to_native_concept: CMS listing of SPC_PE_IMAGE_PAGE_HASHES V1/V2 attrs plus Authenticode flat-table parse (`parsed_page_hash_pairs`; `-` if unwrap fails)

commands.name: verify-pe-page-hashes
commands.maps_to_native_concept: Experimental contiguous raw-file range verify vs parsed page-hash digests — not full WinTrust `/ph`

commands.name: pe-authenticode-ranges
commands.maps_to_native_concept: Diagnostic listing of disjoint PE file ranges hashed by Authenticode image digest (`authenticode-rs` layout); aids `/ph` vs subject-digest reasoning

commands.name: cab-digest
commands.maps_to_native_concept: CAB Authenticode digest hex without PKCS#7

portable_digest_cli.ci: `ci-unix` runs `cargo test -p signtool-digest-cli`; `windows` workflow also builds workspace and runs the same tests.
